question: Define a C++ `struct Book` with members `std::string title`, `std::string author`, and `int publicationYear`. In `main`, create an array of 3 `Book` structs, populate their details, and then iterate through the array to print the details of each book.

test_cases.expected:
Book 1: Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, Author: Douglas Adams, Year: 1979
Book 2: Title: 1984, Author: George Orwell, Year: 0
Book 3: Title: Pride and Prejudice, Author: Jane Austen, Year: 1813

errors: The program does not validate the `publicationYear` member. An invalid year (like 0 in one of the entries) is accepted and printed without any warning or handling, which is a robustness issue against potentially bad data.
